Family: Lead, screening, and consultation
Interview goal: Walk through first contact, screening, and the consultation until enough is known to make a legal and business decision.

Family: Conflict, viability, and acceptance
Interview goal: Explain the conflict result, viability decision, and what makes the firm accept, investigate further, refer, or decline.

Family: Engagement packet, signature, and initial payment
Interview goal: Explain what is sent, what must be signed or paid, how follow-up works, and what makes engagement complete.

Family: Onboarding, initial documents, and matter opening
Interview goal: Explain client onboarding, initial evidence intake, system/file setup, assignments, urgent deadlines, and opening controls.

Family: Additional client information cycle
Interview goal: Explain how missing information is requested, chased, received, reviewed, and incorporated.

Family: Preservation and records acquisition
Interview goal: Explain preservation notices, records requests, follow-up, receipt, review, and how these materials develop the case.

Family: Administrative and agency proceedings
Interview goal: Explain the full agency cycle, deadlines, submissions, conferences, determinations, and transition to litigation.

Family: Pre-litigation demand and negotiation
Interview goal: Explain demand strategy, responses, extensions, offers, settlement, payment, and the decision to file.

Family: Complaint preparation, submission, acceptance, and rejection
Interview goal: Explain complaint approval, filing package, submission, acceptance, rejection correction, and completion proof.

Family: Summons, assignment, and judge decision
Interview goal: Explain summons issuance, assignment notices, judge research, challenge/reassignment choices, and deadlines.

Family: Initial court notices, standing orders, and amended complaint
Interview goal: Explain how initial notices and orders are analysed, calendared, routed, and how amended pleading events are handled.

Family: Service vendor dispatch and attempts
Interview goal: Explain preparation of the service package, vendor instructions, status tracking, failed attempts, and escalation.

Family: Service completion and proof of service
Interview goal: Explain what counts as valid service, proof review, filing, deadline calculations, and correction.

Family: Extension of responsive pleading deadline
Interview goal: Explain when an extension is requested or granted, bargaining considerations, written confirmation, replacement dates, and audit history.

Family: Answers, pleading challenges, arbitration motions, and amended pleadings
Interview goal: Explain the responsive-pleading cycle, review of defenses, threshold motions, meet and confer, rulings, and amended pleadings.

Family: No response and default track
Interview goal: Explain docket checks, default strategy, entry of default, prove-up, rejection correction, and judgment.

Family: Case Management Conference cycle
Interview goal: Explain CMC meet and confer, statement, hearing, order, further conference, exact calendaring, drafting, and closure.

Family: Trial and scheduling dates, cutoffs, OSCs, continuances, and appearance instructions
Interview goal: Explain scheduling orders, dependent dates, compliance hearings, continuances, remote instructions, and recalculation.

Family: Outgoing written discovery
Interview goal: Explain when and why each written-discovery instrument is served, sequencing, proof, response controls, and strategic purpose.

Family: Outgoing depositions and subpoenas
Interview goal: Explain deposition and subpoena strategy, notices, document requests, vendors, dates, and follow-up.

Family: Outgoing expert discovery
Interview goal: Explain expert demand or designation strategy, timing, documents, and approval.

Family: Incoming written discovery
Interview goal: Explain classification by instrument and set, due-date calculations, client work-up, shells, objections, verification, and service.

Family: Incoming depositions, subpoenas, examinations, and expert demands
Interview goal: Explain how each incoming non-written-discovery demand is analysed, calendared, prepared for, objected to, or complied with.

Family: Discovery extension decisions
Interview goal: Explain requests, reciprocity, conditions, refusals, written confirmation, motion-deadline effects, and replacement dates.

Family: Client discovery work-up and plaintiff responses
Interview goal: Explain client answers, document collection, verification, response drafting, production, approval, service, and completion.

Family: Defense and third-party responses, productions, objections, and records
Interview goal: Explain receipt, indexing, deficiency review, privilege/protective-order issues, third-party follow-up, and case-development use.

Family: Depositions, transcripts, and errata
Interview goal: Explain preparation, taking the deposition, transcript handling, errata, impeachment, follow-up, and evidence development.

Family: Discovery no-response, deficiency, meet-and-confer, cure, and IDC
Interview goal: Explain the enforcement ladder from missed or deficient responses through meet and confer, supplemental responses, cure dates, and IDC.

Family: Discovery motions, briefing, rulings, sanctions, and compliance
Interview goal: Explain motion selection, filing, opposition, reply, tentative, hearing, order, sanctions, compliance, and enforcement.

Family: Ex parte, summary judgment, amendment, continuance, and other substantive motions
Interview goal: Explain which motions arise, strategic thresholds, hearing reservation, evidence, and preparation.

Family: Substantive motion briefing and decision cycle
Interview goal: Explain opposition, reply, tentative, hearing, order, notice of entry, and downstream actions.

Family: Mediation and ADR setup, preparation, fees, and event
Interview goal: Explain whether and how ADR is proposed, mediator/vendor choice, date, fees, brief, client preparation, attendance, and event strategy.

Family: Settlement offers, agreement, payment, dismissal, and closing
Interview goal: Explain negotiation, statutory offers, material terms, drafting control, signature, payment, liens, dismissal, and default remedies.

Family: Arbitration from demand through award and judgment
Interview goal: Explain administrator/provider choices, arbitrator selection, scheduling, discovery, hearing, award, judicial review, and judgment.

Family: Expert witness development and depositions
Interview goal: Explain selecting, retaining, preparing, exchanging, receiving, and deposing experts.

Family: Pretrial statements, exchanges, subpoenas, motions in limine, and trial papers
Interview goal: Explain the pretrial production system, coordination, exact filings, exchange, binders, and quality control.

Family: MSC, final conference, trial, daily orders, verdict, and mistrial
Interview goal: Explain event preparation and conduct, settlement opportunities, trial operations, rulings, verdict, and reset after mistrial.

Family: Judgment, costs, fees, and post-trial motions
Interview goal: Explain judgment preparation and entry, notice, costs, fees, post-trial motions, briefing, and rulings.

Family: Appeal cycle
Interview goal: Explain the appeal decision, notice, record, briefing, argument, decision, rehearing/review, and remittitur.

Family: Collection and enforcement
Interview goal: Explain demand, payment monitoring, debtor information, liens, writs, levies, payment plans, defaults, and satisfaction.

Family: Closing, closing letter, archive, and retention
Interview goal: Explain final case and client closure, unresolved duties, closing communication, referrals or relationship handoff, archive, and retention.

Family: New client evidence at any stage
Interview goal: Explain intake, authenticity, urgency, case-theory impact, storage, follow-up, and case/client development.

Family: Court notice, minute order, or signed order at any stage
Interview goal: Explain classification, extraction, deadlines, calendaring, dependent actions, review, and completion.

Family: Opposing-counsel communications and extensions at any stage
Interview goal: Explain how communications are read for commitments, leverage, information, deadlines, response strategy, and memorialisation.

Family: Counsel, contact, and vendor changes or reports
Interview goal: Explain substitutions, service-list/contact changes, invoices/status reports, routing, approval, and system updates.

Family: Failed delivery and missing expected items
Interview goal: Explain how missing or undeliverable items are detected, followed up, escalated, and preserved in the audit trail.

Family: Bankruptcy, death, incapacity, entity status, transfer, or consolidation
Interview goal: Explain immediate holds, legal review, substitutions, stays, venue/status changes, deadline effects, and communications.

Family: Hard-copy document scanned into the case
Interview goal: Explain custody, scanning, OCR, classification, trigger routing, original retention, review, and completion.

Family: Firm-created work product sent
Interview goal: Explain why outgoing work product is a trigger, what response/follow-up cycle it creates, proofs/receipts, and downstream actions.
